comments.body: you should be able to collect JVM, JMX metrics using opentelemetry too. You should look at github.com/SigNoz/signoz. You can try their cloud product signoz.io/pricing and since you have opentelemetry instrumented, you should be able to get started in a few minutes. You can import this dashboard to get JVM metrics github.com/SigNoz/dashboards/tree/main/jvm. Ping me back here if there is some issue. PS: I am one of the maintainers of SigNoz
It's a new tool right?, but our client has paid services for Newrelic, so was looking for solution related to that. but  I can try to propose this new tool as well.  Thank you :)
The New Relic Java agent is not compatible with native compiled Java. You don't specify in your question, but that is the case with many Quarkus apps.
@AndréOnuki yes, We are not using native mode of quarkus right now.
I have tried this library , it says it sending the metrics but I don't see any JVM metrics on newrelic.

answers.body:
Yes, there is. Metrics in Quarkus are currently implemented with Micrometer and you have an OTLP registry (HTTP protocol only) that you can use to send those metrics over. The dependency from Quarkiverse: https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-micrometer-registry/dev/micrometer-registry-otlp.html

<dependency>
   <groupId>io.quarkiverse.micrometer.registry</groupId>
   <artifactId>quarkus-micrometer-registry-otlp</artifactId>
</dependency>

Also there are plans in the Quarkus roadmap around this area, on the Quarkus mailing list: https://groups.google.com/g/quarkus-dev/c/y5-ojIVsa_M/m/4wquJi4bBQAJ

Short answer: The only way is to create widgets similar to the JVM page based on your exported(third-party/OTel) JVM metrics.

Long answer: The JVM metrics page under APM does not show data from third-party agents or instrumentation. When using the NewRelic Java agent, it works as expected, but as soon as we switched to OTel collector, we are unable to see any JVM metrics.

Then, we started exporting JVM metrics through JMX: io.opentelemetry.instrumentation:opentelemetry-jmx-metric

However, the data is now available in the Metric collection in NR, but not still visible on the JVM page. Apparently, the NR Java agent sends some proprietary data that you cannot send :(

title: New Relic APM agent VS New Relic OpenTelemetry for Quarkus microservices monitoring and observability
body:
I have a Quarkus application (consuming/producing messages from multiple Kafka topics) deployed on an AWS Kubernetes cluster. I want to monitor/observe my application. Right now, I am using the Quarkus OpenTelemtry library to send metrics to New Relic which is very straightforward (provide distributed tracing as well with Kafka events out of the box), but it doesn't contain JVM metrics and I can't create SLO for New Relic OpenTelemetry service, there are other features which are not available for OpenTelemetry but available with the Java agent.

Is there a way to get JVM metrics with OpenTelemetry? If not then will it be a good idea to move to java agent?

Please suggest which one should we go with, New Relic OpenTelemtry or New Relic Java agent.
